arxiv_results.title: An Approach to Implement Photovoltaic Self-Consumption and Ramp-Rate Control Algorithm with a Vanadium Redox Flow Battery Day-to-Day Forecast Charging
arxiv_results.abstract: The variability of the solar resource is mainly caused by cloud passing, causing rapid power fluctuations on the output of photovoltaic (PV) systems. This work uses a VRFB to control the power output of the PV installation, maintaining the ramp rate within a non-violation limit and within a battery state of charge (SOC) range. Three EMSs are simulated: self-consumption maximization (SCM), SCM with ramp rate control (SCM+RR), and SCM+RR with weather forecast (SCM+RR+WF). Results show SCM+RR+WF is robust for managing PV+VRFB systems. Over the studied week the strategy successfully controlled 100% of violating power ramps, obtaining a self-consumption ratio (SCR) of 59% and a grid-relief factor (GRF) of 61%.
arxiv_results.relevance_note: PV self-consumption + battery dispatch + SCR/SSR metrics

arxiv_results.title: Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Transformer-Based Reinforcement Learning in Hardware-Constrained Energy Management Systems
arxiv_results.abstract: Transformer-based reinforcement learning for sequential control in residential energy management. Decision Transformer learns effective battery dispatch policies from historical data, thereby increasing photovoltaic self-consumption and reducing electricity costs. Knowledge distillation transfers high-capacity Decision Transformer policies to compact models for embedded deployment. Using the Ausgrid dataset, results show distillation largely preserves control performance while reducing parameter count by up to 96%, inference memory by up to 90%, and inference time by up to 63%.
arxiv_results.relevance_note: Battery dispatch optimization for PV self-consumption using ML — relevant to dispatch strategy comparison
